title: [FIRRTL] Crash in LowerTypes with 0 bit Memory field
body:
The following `fir` input crashes in `LowerTypes`, because of the 0 bit field in Memory.

```python
circuit Queue:
  module Queue :
    input clock : Clock
    input reset : UInt<1>
    output io : { flip enq : { bits : { entry : { reserved : UInt<0>, pscid : UInt<20>, reserved0 : UInt<42>}}}}

    cmem ram : { entry : { reserved : UInt<0>, pscid : UInt<20>, reserved0 : UInt<42>}} [4] @[Decoupled.scala 259:95]
    reg enq_ptr_value : UInt<2>, clock with :
      reset => (reset, UInt<2>("h0")) @[Counter.scala 62:40]

    infer mport MPORT = ram[enq_ptr_value], clock @[Decoupled.scala 273:8]
    MPORT <= io.enq.bits @[Decoupled.scala 273:24]
    node wrap = eq(enq_ptr_value, UInt<2>("h3")) @[Counter.scala 74:24]
    node _value_T = add(enq_ptr_value, UInt<1>("h1")) @[Counter.scala 78:24]
    node _value_T_1 = tail(_value_T, 1) @[Counter.scala 78:24]
    enq_ptr_value <= _value_T_1 @[Counter.scala 78:15]
```
`firtool --repl-seq-mem` 
```
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/ and include the crash backtrace.
```
